name: Hope Of Evansville, Inc
description_short: Non-profit housing and credit counseling organization in Evansville, IN serving low-to-moderate income families with financial education, credit coaching, and homeownership programs.
description_long:
HOPE of Evansville is a non-profit organization headquartered at 900 North Main Street in Evansville, Indiana, dedicated to breaking down barriers to homeownership and affordable housing for low-to-moderate income households. The organization's mission centers on empowering individuals and families to access safe, affordable, and sustainable housing through comprehensive support services and community partnerships.

The organization offers three primary service lines: Fair Shot Financial Education (financial literacy and education programs), Housing Development (affordable housing initiatives), and a First-Time Home Buyer Program specifically designed to prepare prospective homeowners. Beyond these core offerings, HOPE provides credit counseling and housing counseling services to help clients understand credit management, financial planning, and the homeownership process. Their services are designed to equip people with the knowledge and resources necessary to achieve housing stability.

HOPE of Evansville distinguishes itself through its local, mission-driven approach focusing specifically on Evansville and surrounding areas. As a housing nonprofit rather than a for-profit credit repair or lending company, the organization operates without commercial lending incentives, positioning it as an advocacy-focused entity. The organization emphasizes comprehensive preparation through financial education and credit coaching rather than quick-fix solutions, reflecting a long-term empowerment model.

As a free-help non-profit, HOPE operates during standard business hours (Monday-Friday, 8:30 AM-5:00 PM) and requires appointments. The organization is closed weekends and federal holidays. Prospective clients should note that services are appointment-based and the organization's primary strength lies in education and counseling rather than direct financial assistance or lending.
pros: HUD-adjacent non-profit housing counselor organization with mission-driven approach to affordable housing
Offers credit coaching and financial education specifically paired with housing counseling
First-Time Home Buyer Program provides comprehensive preparation for homeownership, not just credit repair
Fair Shot Financial Education program suggests evidence-based financial literacy curriculum
Local organization embedded in Evansville community with direct phone and in-person contact
Services explicitly targeted at low-to-moderate income families with demonstrated need
Multi-service approach addressing housing development, financial education, and credit counseling together
cons: Appointment-only model may create access barriers for those needing immediate assistance
Limited hours (Monday-Friday, 8:30 AM-5:00 PM) exclude working people unable to take daytime appointments
Website lacks detailed program descriptions, eligibility requirements, or timeline information
No indication of whether organization offers HUD-certified counselor credentials or NFCC certification status
No pricing, cost structure, or explicit statement that services are free (though non-profit status suggests this)
best_for: First-time home buyers in Evansville/surrounding Indiana areas seeking comprehensive preparation
Low-to-moderate income households wanting to improve credit and financial literacy before homeownership
Individuals needing credit coaching paired with housing counseling rather than credit dispute services
Community members looking for local, non-profit financial guidance without commercial lending agendas
diagnosis: HOPE of Evansville is best for low-to-moderate income individuals and families in the Evansville area preparing for homeownership who benefit from integrated financial education, credit coaching, and housing counseling. The main caveat is that this is an appointment-based, business-hours-only service with limited details online—prospective clients must call (812) 423-3169 to learn specific eligibility, costs (likely free), and availability rather than applying online.
services: Credit counseling for low-to-moderate income families
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
